evals.prompt: I want to structure a new Go microservice following Clean Architecture. What are the top-level directories and what goes in each?
evals.expected_output: cmd/ for entry points, internal/domain for interfaces and entities, internal/adapter for HTTP handlers and DB repos, pkg/ for shared libraries. Dependencies flow inward only — adapters depend on domain, not vice versa.

evals.prompt: My domain entity needs to call a database query. Should I import the DB package directly in the domain layer?
evals.expected_output: No. Domain must have zero external dependencies. Define a repository interface in the domain layer; the adapter (infrastructure) layer implements it. Inject via constructor.

evals.prompt: I have a Go service that handles orders, inventory, and notifications. Should this all be in one service struct?
evals.expected_output: No god services. Split into single-responsibility components: OrderService, InventoryService, NotificationService. Wire them together in main.go via DI.

should_not_trigger: How do I write a goroutine in Go?
Write a table-driven test in Go
